title: 10 Herbal Tea Remedies to Cure a Hangover
intro_desc: Herbal teas for hangover are natural remedies that help alleviate the symptoms of a hangover, such as nausea, headaches, and fatigue, by soothing the stomach, reducing inflammation, and replenishing essential nutrients. The best herbal teas for hangover include Zingiber officinale, Ginkgo biloba, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Cinchona officinalis, Camellia sinensis, Panax ginseng, Artemisia absinthium, Matricaria chamomilla, Valeriana officinalis, and Rosmarinus officinalis. Without these remedies, hangovers can negatively impact daily lives, causing missed work, social isolation, and decreased productivity.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tea, also known as ginger tea, is a popular remedy for hangover. The reason for its effectiveness lies in its ability to calm the stomach and alleviate nausea. When we drink too much alcohol, it irritates our stomach lining, leading to vomiting and stomach pain. Ginger tea works by reducing inflammation in the stomach and soothing the digestive system. It also helps to break down and absorb the toxins and waste products caused by alcohol consumption. Furthermore, ginger has natural pain-relieving properties, which can help to ease headaches and body aches commonly associated with hangovers. Additionally, ginger tea has been shown to have antioxidant properties, which can help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the body caused by excessive drinking. This makes Zingiber officinale tea a valuable natural remedy for people looking to alleviate hangover symptoms.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerols, commonly found in peppermint, can help alleviate hangover symptoms by reducing nausea and inflammation caused by excessive alcohol consumption.
Shogaols are good for hangover because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nausea and vomiting caused by alcohol consumption.
Polyphenols, found in peppermint, are good for hangover because they help to reduce nausea and inflammation in the body, and also ease digestive issues.

Tannins, also known as peppermint, are good for hangover because they help to reduce nausea and inflammation in the stomach by acting as an antispasmodic and an anti-inflammatory agent, which can help to alleviate the symptoms of a hangover, such as stomach pain and vomiting.

remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for hangover
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a refreshing Mentha x piperita herbal tea that helps with hangover, start by gathering a handful of fresh peppermint leaves and a few sprigs of spearmint. Rinse them under cold running water, then gently pat them dry with a clean towel. Next, place the leaves in a teapot or a large mug and pour in 1 cup of boiling water. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the oils and flavorings from the herbs to infuse into the water. Once the tea has cooled slightly, strain it into a separate container to remove the leaves. You can drink this tea hot or iced, sweetened with a bit of honey if desired. As you sip the tea, the menthol and menthone in peppermint and spearmint may help alleviate nausea and headaches, while their cooling properties can also soothe your stomach and calm your mind.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tea is a popular remedy for hangovers. The leaves of the Ginkgo biloba tree have been used in traditional medicine for thousands of years. The tea is said to help alleviate hangover symptoms like headaches, nausea, and fatigue. This is because Ginkgo biloba has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the brain. It also improves blood flow to the brain, which can help ease the pain and discomfort associated with hangovers. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba has been shown to help reduce the severity of withdrawal symptoms from alcohol, making it a potentially useful aid for those trying to cut back or quit drinking. The tea's ability to help the body recover from the effects of alcohol may be due in part to its ability to increase the production of antioxidants in the body, which can help neutralize the damaging effects of free radicals caused by excessive drinking. Drinking Ginkgo biloba tea after a night of heavy drinking may help alleviate some of the unpleasant symptoms associated with hangovers.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin, or peppermint, is good for hangover because it helps reduce inflammation and nausea in the body caused by excessive drinking, and also improves blood flow to the brain to alleviate headaches and fatigue.

Kaempferol is good for hangover because it has strong antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce the damage to the body caused by excessive drinking, and its ability to calm the stomach and alleviate nausea makes it a natural remedy for hangover relief.
Flavonoids, specifically found in peppermint, are good for hangover because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce headaches and nausea caused by alcohol consumption.

remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal teas for hangover
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing hangover tea with Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, you'll need some fresh peppermint leaves, a teapot, and a strainer. First, gently wash the peppermint leaves in cold water to remove any dirt or impurities. Next, add one tablespoon of fresh peppermint leaves to a teapot and pour in 8 ounces of boiling water. Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to release into the water. You can also add a slice of lemon to enhance the flavor. Once the tea has steeped, carefully pour it into a cup through a strainer to catch the peppermint leaves. Finally, enjoy the tea while it's still warm, and feel free to add a bit of honey if you prefer it sweeter. This refreshing tea is known to help alleviate nausea and ease stomach discomfort, making it a great remedy for hangover relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis tea is a natural remedy that can help with hangover symptoms. The plant contains a compound called quinine, which has anti-inflammatory properties. When you drink too much alcohol, your body gets dehydrated and your stomach hurts. Quinine in Cinchona officinalis tea helps to reduce inflammation and ease stomach pain. It also helps to replenish fluids and electrolytes in your body, which can become imbalanced after heavy drinking. Additionally, the tea has a mild diuretic effect, which can help to flush out toxins and excess fluids from your system. As a result, Cinchona officinalis tea can provide relief from hangover symptoms such as headaches, nausea, and fatigue. Drinking a cup of this tea after a night of drinking may help to alleviate these symptoms and make you feel better faster.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quinine is good for hangover because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce nausea and pain, making it easier to recover from a night of excessive drinking.

remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal teas for hangover
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Mentha x piperita (Peppermint) herbal tea for hangover relief, start by gathering fresh peppermint leaves or dried peppermint tea bags. If using fresh leaves, rinse them gently with cold water to remove any dirt. Next, boil water in a kettle or on the stovetop, then let it cool for a minute or two to avoid burning the peppermint. Place one teaspoon of fresh leaves or one tea bag into a cup, and pour the cooled water over them. Let the tea steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the peppermint oils to release and infuse into the water. Strain the tea into another cup if using loose leaves, and add honey or sugar to taste if desired. Drink the peppermint tea slowly, sipping it over 10-15 minutes, to help soothe stomach cramps, ease nausea, and calm the digestive system, providing relief from hangover symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian root, is a herbal tea that has been used for centuries to help with hangover symptoms. The tea works by reducing inflammation and calming the nervous system. When we drink too much alcohol, our body releases stress hormones like adrenaline, which can cause headaches, nausea, and fatigue. Valerian root tea helps to slow down the release of these hormones, giving our body time to recover. It also contains antioxidants that help to neutralize the toxic effects of alcohol on our body. Additionally, valerian root tea is a natural sedative, which can help to calm our stomach and reduce nausea. When we drink valerian root tea, it can help to reduce the severity of hangover symptoms, making it a popular remedy for those who like to have a good time but don't want to suffer the next day.

Tannins, also known as peppermint, are good for hangover because they help reduce inflammation and nausea in the stomach by calming the digestive system and soothing the stomach lining, making them a potential natural remedy for hangover relief.

Valerenal is good for hangover because it helps to reduce inflammation and pain in the brain and body, and also has calming effects that can ease headaches and nausea caused by overdrinking.

remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal teas for hangover
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Mentha x piperita herbal tea that helps with hangover, start by gathering fresh peppermint leaves or dried peppermint powder. If using fresh leaves, choose ones that are free of pests and diseases, and pick a few sprigs for your tea. You can also use dried peppermint powder, which can be found in most health food stores or online. Next, boil a cup of water and let it cool for a minute or two. Add 1-2 teaspoons of fresh peppermint leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried peppermint powder to the water, and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea into a cup and add honey or lemon to taste. Drink the tea as soon as you wake up after a night of drinking to help alleviate hangover symptoms like nausea, headaches, and stomach cramps. Peppermint tea has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe your stomach and calm your mind, making it a popular remedy for hangovers.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata tea, also known as passionflower tea, is a natural remedy for hangovers. This tea has been used for centuries to calm the mind and soothe the body. The active compounds in passionflower, such as flavonoids and alkaloids, have a sedative effect that can help reduce anxiety and stress caused by a hangover. These compounds can also help slow down the release of cortisol, the hormone that makes us feel stressed and irritable. By reducing cortisol levels, passionflower tea can help ease hangover symptoms like nausea, headaches, and fatigue. Additionally, passionflower tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain associated with a hangover. Drinking passionflower tea may also help regulate sleep patterns, which can be disrupted by a hangover. As the tea works to calm the mind and soothe the body, it can help speed up the recovery process and make hangovers more manageable.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing Mentha x piperita, or peppermint, herbal tea to help with hangover, start by gathering a handful of fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or a local store. Rinse the leaves with cold water to remove any dirt or impurities, then chop them into smaller pieces. Next, boil a kettle of water and let it cool for a minute or two. To help alleviate hangover symptoms, drink the peppermint tea hot or iced, and consider repeating the process every few hours to stay hydrated and feel better.

Ginseng tea has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to constrict blood vessels and ease the pain. Additionally, ginseng tea can help to improve circulation, which can aid in the removal of toxins from the body. This can help to reduce nausea and fatigue, two common symptoms of hangovers. Ginseng tea also contains antioxidants that can help to protect the body from oxidative stress caused by excessive drinking. Furthermore, ginseng is believed to have a balancing effect on the body's energy and can help to regulate our body's natural rhythms, which can be disrupted by excessive drinking.

remedies.remedy_desc: Artemisia absinthium, also known as wormwood, is a natural remedy that's been used for centuries to help with hangovers. The tea made from its leaves contains a compound called thujone, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. When you drink too much alcohol, your body suffers from dehydration and inflammation. Thujone in wormwood tea helps to reduce this inflammation and ease the pain in your head and stomach. Additionally, wormwood tea is a natural diuretic, which means it helps your body to get rid of excess fluids, reducing the chances of dehydration. Drinking wormwood tea after a night of drinking can also help to calm your stomach and reduce nausea. Some people even claim that wormwood tea helps to reduce the hangover symptoms like dizziness and fatigue. However, it's essential to note that wormwood tea should be consumed in moderation, as excessive consumption can have negative effects.

supplementary_best: A combination of herbal teas that can help remedy a hangover includes Zingiber officinale (Ginger), Valeriana officinalis (Valerian root), and Mentha x piperita (Peppermint). Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce nausea and stomach pain caused by excessive drinking. Valerian root is known for its calming effects, which can help alleviate headaches and fatigue often experienced after a hangover. Peppermint tea has a cooling effect on the stomach and can help ease nausea and indigestion. This combination works well because the anti-inflammatory properties of ginger can help reduce pain and inflammation in the stomach, while the calming effects of valerian root can help relax the body and mind. Meanwhile, peppermint tea can help soothe the stomach and reduce nausea. Drinking this combination of teas can help alleviate some of the symptoms associated with a hangover, such as nausea, stomach pain, and headaches.
supplementary_avoid: If you have a hangover, there are some herbal teas that you should avoid. One of these teas is the one made from Ephedra sinica, also known as ma huang. This herb can increase heart rate and blood pressure, making it a bad choice when you're already feeling dehydrated and possibly experiencing a pounding headache. Another tea to avoid is the one made from Yohimbe bark, which is known to increase blood pressure and heart rate even more. This can worsen dehydration and cause other complications. Additionally, tea made from Aristolochia species, such as the birthwort plant, should be avoided due to its potential to cause kidney damage and interact with medications. Also, avoid tea made from Cola nitida, which can cause more dehydration and increase heart rate. Finally, tea made from Ephedrae herbae should be avoided due to its similar effects to Ephedra sinica, increasing heart rate and blood pressure.
supplementary_other_remedies: Ginger has been used for centuries to help with nausea and stomach issues. It works by reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles in the stomach, making it easier to digest food. Some people eat ginger in the form of fresh roots, while others use ginger candies or ginger ale to get a dose.  Coconut water is another natural remedy that can help with hangover symptoms. It contains electrolytes like potassium and sodium, which are lost when we drink too much alcohol. Drinking coconut water helps to replenish these electrolytes and rehydrate the body.  Activated charcoal can also be used to help with hangover symptoms. It works by absorbing toxins in the stomach and small intestine, which can help to reduce nausea and vomiting. Some people take activated charcoal supplements or drink it in the form of a drink.  Epsom salt baths are another way to help with hangover symptoms. The magnesium in Epsom salt can help to relax the muscles and reduce inflammation, making it easier to feel better. Some people soak in Epsom salt baths for 15-20 minutes to get relief from hangover symptoms.  Bee pollen is a natural remedy that has been used to help with hangover symptoms. It contains vitamins and minerals like B12 and iron, which can help to replenish the body after a night of drinking. Some people take bee pollen supplements or add it to their food to get a dose.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who have a hangover often experience other ailments. One common issue is dehydration, which can lead to headaches, dry mouth, and fatigue. Medicinal herbs like peppermint and ginger can help with dehydration. Peppermint helps to cool the body and reduce inflammation, while ginger is known for its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve nausea. Another common issue is stomach upset, which can cause nausea and vomiting. Herbs like chamomile and licorice root can soothe the stomach and reduce inflammation. Chamomile has anti-inflammatory properties that can calm the stomach, while licorice root can help to protect the stomach lining and reduce inflammation. Some people also experience migraines, which can be caused by dehydration and changes in blood flow. Herbs like feverfew and willow bark contain compounds that can help to reduce pain and inflammation. Feverfew has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce migraine symptoms, while willow bark contains salicylic acid, a natural pain reliever.
